# Contracts

## getKgSyncState and setKgSyncState KV API for transient state tracking

[Trigger]: When implementing features that need to track transient sync state across sessions (e.g., observer last-run timestamp, incremental scan progress). [Detail]: The kg_sync_state table (key TEXT PRIMARY KEY, value TEXT) is accessed via getKgSyncState(db, key) and setKgSyncState(db, key, value), both exported from src/db/knowledge-sync.js. These helpers abstract away raw SQL and provide consistent KV semantics. [Consequence]: Direct SQL queries to kg_sync_state bypass the abstraction and fail if schema changes. Importing from wrong modules causes MODULE_NOT_FOUND.

_Source: `src/db/knowledge-sync.js`_

_Tags: `backend`, `database`_

## callClaude(prompt, model, opts) wrapper does not support --effort flag

[Trigger]: When modifying the callClaude wrapper in src/knowledge/extract.js or invoking it from scan.js. [Detail]: The callClaude function signature accepts an opts parameter that previously included `{ effort: 'max' }`, but the wrapper never actually constructs a --effort CLI argument. The opts object is only used internally (e.g., for other future options), not passed to the spawn args array. [Consequence]: Passing `{ effort: 'max' }` to callClaude has no effect on the actual Claude CLI invocation. To add effort control in the future, both the wrapper implementation and the prompt must be updated together.

_Source: `src/knowledge/extract.js`_

_Tags: `backend`, `api`_

## savePlan(db, id, planMd, handoffPrompt, runId) persists generated plan linked to pipeline_runs for cost tracking

[Trigger]: When plan generation spawns a background Claude CLI process and needs to store the result asynchronously. [Detail]: The savePlan helper updates a daily_reviews row with four fields: plan_md (the generated text), handoff_prompt (the context passed to Claude), plan_status='done' (success state), and plan_run_id (FK to the pipeline_runs table row that tracked the Claude CLI invocation). This links the generated plan to cost and token data recorded in pipeline_runs. [Consequence]: Without the runId reference, cost analysis cannot attribute plan generation expenses to specific daily review suggestions, inflating operational cost tracking.

_Source: `src/review/queries.js`_

_Tags: `database`, `api`_

## GET /api/daily-reviews/:id/plan-status returns {plan_status, plan_error, plan_run_id} for polling

[Trigger]: When plan generation is triggered asynchronously and the frontend needs to poll for completion status. [Detail]: The GET /api/daily-reviews/:id/plan-status endpoint returns a JSON object with three fields: plan_status (null|running|done|error), plan_error (error message string or null), and plan_run_id (reference to pipeline_runs table or null). The frontend uses this endpoint to determine whether to show completion UI, error state, or keep polling. [Consequence]: Frontend polling logic depends on these three fields being present and correctly updated as the background process progresses.

_Source: `src/routes/daily-reviews.js`_

_Tags: `api`, `frontend`_

## savePlan stores plan_md, handoff_prompt, plan_status, and links to pipeline_runs via run_id

[Trigger]: When plan generation spawns a detached child process and needs to persist results after Claude CLI completes. [Detail]: savePlan(db, id, planMd, handoffPrompt, runId) updates a daily_reviews row by setting plan_md (the generated plan markdown), handoff_prompt (the context passed to Claude), plan_status='done', and run_id (FK to pipeline_runs for cost tracking). [Consequence]: Plan status polling retrieves these fields from the daily_reviews table; without populating them, clients see null values even after the operation completes.

_Source: `src/review/queries.js`_

_Tags: `backend`, `database`_

## Plan generation endpoint returns 202 Accepted with task ID for polling, not blocking response

[Trigger]: When POST /api/daily-reviews/:id/plan/generate is triggered from the UI. [Detail]: The route handler spawns a detached child process immediately and returns 202 {task_id, status: 'running'} without waiting for completion. The child process runs generatePlan() asynchronously, updating pipeline_runs and daily_reviews tables as it progresses. Frontend polls GET /api/daily-reviews/:id/plan-status to check completion (plan_status='done' or 'error'). [Consequence]: Blocking HTTP responses would timeout for long-running plan generation (300-600s); 202 pattern unblocks Fastify and lets frontend poll for results.

_Source: `src/routes/daily-reviews.js`_

_Tags: `api`, `backend`_

## updatePipelineRun helper required for all async Claude CLI invocations

[Trigger]: When implementing features that spawn detached Claude CLI processes (plan generation, daily review). [Detail]: All three LLM pipelines (knowledge extraction, scan, daily review) follow insert-then-update pattern: insertPipelineRun(db, ...) at start with status='running', then updatePipelineRun(db, id, {status: 'success|error', input_tokens, output_tokens, duration_ms, error}) on completion. Plan generation follows the same pattern to track costs and status separately from user event costs. [Consequence]: Missing updatePipelineRun call leaves pipeline_runs rows with status='running' forever, blocking shutdown cleanup and masking failures.

_Source: `src/db/pipeline-runs.js`_

_Tags: `backend`, `database`_

## GET /api/daily-reviews/:id/plan-status returns {plan_status, plan_error, plan_run_id}

[Trigger]: When the frontend polls for plan generation status after submitting a plan request. [Detail]: The endpoint returns an object with three fields: plan_status (null|'running'|'done'|'error'), plan_error (error message if status='error', null otherwise), and plan_run_id (ID for cost tracking in pipeline_runs table). The plan_status='running' indicates the child process is still executing; 'done' means plan_md and handoff_prompt have been populated; 'error' indicates the Claude CLI invocation failed. [Consequence]: Frontend polling logic depends on these exact field names and null/string values; missing fields or unexpected status values cause polling to stall.

_Source: `src/routes/daily-reviews.js`_

_Tags: `api`, `frontend`_

## savePlan helper stores plan state in daily_reviews table with tracking columns

[Trigger]: When plan generation completes asynchronously and needs to persist the generated plan. [Detail]: savePlan(db, id, planMd, handoffPrompt, runId) updates the daily_reviews record with: plan_md (generated markdown), handoff_prompt (context passed to Claude), plan_status='done', plan_generated_at=NOW. The runId links the plan_runs row to daily_reviews for cost tracking. [Consequence]: Omitting runId or plan_status prevents cost tracking and breaks correlation between plan generation and the original suggestion.

_Source: `src/review/queries.js`_

_Tags: `backend`, `database`, `contract`_

## updatePipelineRun helper required for tracking async Claude CLI invocations

[Trigger]: When plan generation or other async Claude CLI operations complete asynchronously and need to record final status. [Detail]: insertPipelineRun inserts a row with status='running' at the start. updatePipelineRun(db, id, fields) completes the tracking by accepting: status, error, input_tokens, output_tokens, duration_ms. The plan generation subprocess calls updatePipelineRun to record the final Claude CLI execution metrics. [Consequence]: Omitting updatePipelineRun call leaves pipeline_runs entries in 'running' status indefinitely, polluting the pipeline run history.

_Source: `src/db/pipeline-runs.js`_

_Tags: `backend`, `database`, `architecture`_

## Observer state tracking uses per-project keys in kg_sync_state table

[Trigger]: When designing the observer subsystem to track incremental event processing across projects. [Detail]: The kg_sync_state KV table stores observer state with keys following the pattern observer_last_run_at_${project_id} to track the latest processed event timestamp per project. This allows the observer to resume processing from where it stopped for each project independently without global state collision. [Consequence]: Observer implementations must construct keys with the exact pattern observer_last_run_at_${project_id} when calling getKgSyncState/setKgSyncState, or observer state will be lost across restarts.

_Source: `src/db/knowledge-sync.js`_

_Tags: `contract`, `backend`_

## Knowledge scan.js passes effort: 'max' to callClaude

[Trigger]: When modifying the knowledge extraction pipeline or understanding quality tuning options. [Detail]: The scan.js module explicitly passes {effort: 'max'} to callClaude() on line 166, controlling Claude's reasoning depth for cold-start scans. The memory entry claiming effort is unused in all pipelines is outdated — scan.js DOES use it. [Consequence]: If effort parameter is removed, cold-start knowledge scans will lose reasoning depth, reducing detection of subtle project patterns and conventions.

_Source: `src/knowledge/scan.js`_

_Tags: `backend`, `config`_

## callClaude timeout extended to 120s for plan generation with large handoff prompts

[Trigger]: When plan generation invokes callClaude() to synthesize an implementation plan from daily review context. [Detail]: src/knowledge/extract.js exports callClaude(prompt, model='opus', opts={}) which accepts an opts.timeout parameter in milliseconds. Plan generation uses a 120s timeout (vs the default) because handoff prompts from daily review context can be large (500 KB+). The timeout must account for both token generation and Claude's internal processing time. [Consequence]: If timeout is too short, plan generation fails with ETIMEDOUT — if too long, the API blocks for 2+ minutes per invocation.

_Source: `src/knowledge/extract.js`_

_Tags: `backend`, `config`_

## Plan generation endpoint returns task ID for polling, not blocking response

[Trigger]: When POST /api/daily-reviews/:id/plan/generate is triggered from the UI. [Detail]: The route spawns a detached child process and returns 202 Accepted with {task_id} immediately. The frontend polls GET /api/daily-reviews/:id?includePolling=true to check plan_status. This unblocks the HTTP response while plan generation continues in the background for 300-600 seconds. [Consequence]: If the route blocked on execFileSync, Fastify event loop would stall and concurrent requests would hang.

_Source: `src/routes/daily-reviews.js`_

_Tags: `api`_

## savePlan stores generated plan and handoff context linked to daily review

[Trigger]: When plan generation completes asynchronously and needs to persist results. [Detail]: The savePlan(db, id, planMd, handoffPrompt, runId) function updates the daily_reviews table with plan_md, handoff_prompt, and plan_status='done' columns. The runId links the plan generation to the pipeline_runs table for cost tracking. The handoffPrompt preserves the context used to generate the plan for reproducibility. [Consequence]: Without storing the handoff context, plans become disconnected from their generation context and cannot be re-run or debugged.

_Source: `src/review/queries.js`_

_Tags: `database`_

## updatePipelineRun helper extends pipeline_runs tracking for async operation completion

[Trigger]: When an async Claude CLI operation (plan generation, daily review) completes and needs status update. [Detail]: The updatePipelineRun(db, id, fields) function updates a pipeline_runs row created by insertPipelineRun. It accepts a subset of fields: status, error, input_tokens, output_tokens, duration_ms. This is separate from and used after insertPipelineRun for the insert-then-update pattern. [Consequence]: Without this pattern, async operations would have no way to record their completion status and resource usage after the initial row insertion.

_Source: `src/db/pipeline-runs.js`_

_Tags: `database`_

## Observer state tracking uses getKgSyncState and setKgSyncState utilities

[Trigger]: When implementing the observer subprocess to track progress per project incrementally. [Detail]: The getKgSyncState(db, key) and setKgSyncState(db, key, value) functions from src/db/knowledge-sync.js provide a KV interface to the kg_sync_state table. Key format for observer: `observer_last_run_at_${project_id}` stores ISO timestamp of the last processed event. Missing keys return undefined; values are always strings and must be explicitly serialized/deserialized. [Consequence]: Observer can resume from the last event per project without re-processing; values must be ISO timestamp strings not raw numbers.

_Source: `src/db/knowledge-sync.js`_

_Tags: `backend`, `database`_

## updatePipelineRun helper required for async Claude CLI invocations

[Trigger]: When implementing features that spawn detached Claude CLI processes (plan generation, daily review extensions). [Detail]: updatePipelineRun(db, id, fields) updates an existing pipeline_runs row after async completion, separate from insertPipelineRun which inserts at start with status='running'. The helper validates allowed fields (status, error, input_tokens, output_tokens, duration_ms, started_at) to prevent injection. All async Claude CLI features follow the insert-then-update pattern: insertPipelineRun on spawn, updatePipelineRun on completion. [Consequence]: Async operations can be tracked end-to-end without blocking the HTTP response.

_Source: `src/db/pipeline-runs.js`_

_Tags: `database`, `backend`_

## getConfig helper returns fresh config from disk per call, not cached

[Trigger]: When a new feature needs to respect configuration values that might change between requests without server restart. [Detail]: src/lib/config.js exports getConfig() which reads config.json from disk on every invocation. The function loads the file fresh and merges with DEFAULT_CONFIG to provide all known keys with fallback defaults. This pattern allows config tuning in production without restarting the server. [Consequence]: Config values like plan_generation_timeout_ms can be changed and will take effect on the next request.

_Source: `src/lib/config.js`_

_Tags: `config`, `backend`_

## updatePipelineRun extends pipeline_runs tracking for async operation completion

[Trigger]: When an async Claude CLI operation (plan generation, daily review extension) completes and needs state tracking. [Detail]: updatePipelineRun(db, id, fields) updates a row in pipeline_runs with final status, error message, token counts, and duration. Only fields in the whitelist (status, error, input_tokens, output_tokens, duration_ms) are accepted. The function is idempotent. [Consequence]: All async Claude invocations must call insertPipelineRun first, then updatePipelineRun on completion. Omitting either step loses operational visibility and cost tracking.

_Source: `src/db/pipeline-runs.js`_

_Tags: `database`, `backend`_

## savePlan signature stores plan with runId to link to pipeline_runs for cost tracking

[Trigger]: When plan generation completes and needs to persist results to the database. [Detail]: savePlan(db, id, planMd, handoffPrompt, runId) updates the daily_reviews row by setting plan_md, handoff_prompt, plan_status='done', and plan_run_id=runId. The runId foreign key links to the pipeline_runs table that tracks the Claude CLI invocation cost and duration. [Consequence]: Omitting the runId parameter breaks cost attribution for plan generation—queries cannot correlate plan_md results to their API cost.

_Source: `src/review/queries.js`_

_Tags: `backend`, `database`_

## Plan generation consumes daily_reviews suggestion JSON with title, description, target_type fields

[Trigger]: When plan generation is triggered from a daily review suggestion detail view. [Detail]: The generatePlan(db, suggestionId, opts) function receives a suggestion ID and queries the daily_reviews table to fetch title, description, and target_type fields. These suggestion fields are used as seed context for building the plan prompt. The suggestion's projects field determines which project context to load. [Consequence]: Changing daily_reviews schema (adding/removing fields that generatePlan relies on) requires corresponding updates to src/review/plan.js buildSuggestionPrompt() function.

_Source: `src/review/plan.js`_

_Tags: `backend`, `api`_

## Plan generation implementation spans schema, config, queries, helpers, routes, and frontend coordination

[Trigger]: When implementing a multi-layer feature that requires changes across database, configuration, business logic, API, and UI. [Detail]: The 10-step pattern (schema → config → helpers → queries → timeout → tests → implementation → routes → tests → frontend) ensures each layer has its dependencies available before dependent code runs. Skipping or reordering steps causes cascading failures — routes fail before queries exist, tests fail before modules exist. [Consequence]: Non-systematic implementation leads to circular dependency issues, incomplete tests, and wasted refactoring.

_Source: `src/review/plan.js`_

_Tags: `backend`, `testing`_
